description: ABoard - Cross-platform smart clipboard desktop app (Tauri v2 + SolidJS) with local AI integration. Captures clipboard content, provides persistent history, AI-powered classification/tagging/summarization, translation, rewriting, format tools, and semantic search.

userStories.title: Scaffold Tauri v2 project with SolidJS frontend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project scaffolded with Tauri v2, SolidJS, TypeScript, TailwindCSS v4, and Vite so that we have a working development environment.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Tauri v2 project with cargo tauri init (or manual setup)
Configure Vite with vite-plugin-solid for SolidJS
Add TailwindCSS v4 with @tailwindcss/vite plugin
Create src/index.tsx entry point rendering App component into #root
Add tauri.conf.json with product name 'ABoard', identifier 'com.aboard.app'
npm run dev starts both Vite and Tauri without errors
cargo check passes in src-tauri/
npx tsc --noEmit passes

userStories.title: Design system tokens and CSS utilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CSS design tokens and utility classes for a consistent glassmorphism design language across the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/styles/design-tokens.css with CSS custom properties for: colors (dark theme), typography, spacing (4px base), border radii, glassmorphism (blur, bg, border), shadows, transitions
Create src/styles/index.css importing tailwindcss, design-tokens.css, and defining utility classes: .glass, .glass-subtle, .glass-card, .window-bg, .transition-smooth, .transition-spring
Add keyframe animations: slideIn, contextMenuIn, popupIn, scaleIn with corresponding .animate-* classes
Add .hover-lift utility class
Import styles in src/index.tsx
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SQLite database schema with FTS5
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the SQLite database layer with clipboard_items table, FTS5 full-text search, indexes, and migration support.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src-tauri/src/db.rs with DbState struct wrapping Mutex<Connection>

clipboard_items table: id TEXT PK, content_type TEXT NOT NULL, content TEXT NOT NULL, hash TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, timestamp INTEGER NOT NULL, metadata TEXT DEFAULT '{}', pinned INTEGER DEFAULT 0, pinned_at INTEGER, ai_type TEXT, ai_tags TEXT, ai_summary TEXT
model_metadata table: id TEXT PK, name, filename, file_path, file_size, status, downloaded_at, context_length, description
FTS5 virtual table clipboard_items_fts covering content, ai_tags, ai_summary columns
INSERT/DELETE/UPDATE triggers keeping FTS in sync
Indexes on timestamp DESC and (pinned, pinned_at DESC)
ALTER TABLE migration for ai_type, ai_tags, ai_summary columns (compatible with existing DBs)
FTS rebuild migration to add ai_tags and ai_summary to existing FTS index
init_db(app) function creates schema and registers DbState as Tauri managed state
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Clipboard monitoring core with SHA256 dedup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Rust clipboard monitoring that detects changes, deduplicates via SHA256, persists to SQLite, and emits events to the frontend.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src-tauri/src/clipboard.rs with ClipboardItem struct (id, content_type, content, hash, timestamp, metadata, pinned, pinned_at, ai_type, ai_tags, ai_summary) with serde derive
ClipboardContent enum: Text(String), Image(String), FilePaths(String)
compute_hash function using sha2 SHA256 returning hex string
start_monitoring(app) spawns async polling loop (200ms interval) using tauri_plugin_clipboard_manager
On clipboard change: compute hash, skip if duplicate (query DB), generate UUID, insert to DB, emit 'clipboard-update' Tauri event
detect_content_type function distinguishing 'text' and 'file-paths'
MAX_CONTENT_SIZE = 10MB, content exceeding this is skipped
toggle_monitoring Tauri command to pause/resume
Register toggle_monitoring in invoke_handler
cargo check passes

userStories.title: System tray with menu items
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to run in the system tray with a menu to show the window, pause monitoring, or quit.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src-tauri/src/tray.rs with setup_tray function
Tray menu items: 'Show Window', 'Pause/Resume Monitoring' (toggle), 'Quit'
Left-click tray icon shows main window
tray-icon feature enabled in Tauri config
Tray icon configured in tauri.conf.json with iconPath and iconAsTemplate
Call setup_tray in setup() block of lib.rs run()
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Global shortcuts and window management
userStories.description: As a user, I want Cmd+Shift+V to toggle a floating popup and the main window to hide (not close) on close.
acceptanceCriteria: Register Cmd+Shift+V global shortcut in setup() using tauri_plugin_global_shortcut
On press: toggle floating window visibility, center on cursor position before showing
Floating window config in tauri.conf.json: label 'floating', width 360, height 420, no decorations, transparent, alwaysOnTop, initially hidden
Main window close-to-tray: onMount handler prevents close and calls hide() instead
Register Cmd+Shift+J global shortcut for quick cycle (placeholder for now)
CycleState struct in lib.rs: index (usize), last_cycle (Option<Instant>)
Register CycleState as Tauri managed state
cargo check passes

userStories.title: DB commands for history, pin, delete, and insert
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Tauri commands for loading history, searching, pinning, deleting, and inserting clipboard items.
acceptanceCriteria: get_history(state, offset, limit): returns Vec<ClipboardItem>, pinned first then by timestamp DESC
search_history(state, query, offset, limit): FTS5 MATCH search, sanitizes query, wraps in double quotes
delete_items(state, ids): deletes by IDs with UUID format validation, triggers handle FTS cleanup
pin_item(state, id): sets pinned=1, pinned_at=now
unpin_item(state, id): sets pinned=0, pinned_at=NULL
get_pinned(state): returns all pinned items
update_item_content(state, id, content): updates content text
insert_clipboard_item(state, id, content_type, content, hash, timestamp, metadata): INSERT OR IGNORE
update_ai_metadata(state, id, ai_type, ai_tags, ai_summary): validates ai_type enum, validates ai_tags JSON array
Register all commands in invoke_handler in lib.rs
row_to_item helper parses all 11 columns
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Frontend clipboard store with Tauri event listeners
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a SolidJS reactive store that manages clipboard items and communicates with the Rust backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/stores/clipboard.ts with ClipboardItem interface matching Rust struct
Signals: items, setItems, loading, searchQuery, setSearchQuery, selectedId, setSelectedId, selectedIds, setSelectedIds
toggleSelect(id), clearSelection(), selectAll() functions
loadHistory(offset, limit): calls invoke('get_history')
searchHistory(query): calls invoke('search_history')
deleteItems(ids), pinItem(id), unpinItem(id): invoke corresponding commands
addItem(item): adds to reactive signal with hash dedup
startClipboardListener(): listens to 'clipboard-update' and 'ai-processed' Tauri events, updates signals reactively
stopClipboardListener(): unregisters listeners
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: paste_to_active command for macOS

On macOS (cfg target_os): uses core_graphics CGEvent to simulate Cmd down, V down, V up, Cmd up
On other platforms: only writes to clipboard (no keystroke simulation)

userStories.title: InferenceProvider trait and AI module setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the AI module foundation with the InferenceProvider trait, shared types, and config persistence.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src-tauri/src/ai/mod.rs with InferenceProvider async trait (infer, name, is_available)
InferenceRequest struct: prompt, system_prompt, max_tokens, temperature, top_p
InferenceResponse struct: text, tokens_used, provider, duration_ms
InferenceAutoResponse struct: response, routing_decision
LocalProviderStatus struct: ollama_available, llamacpp_available, detected_models
AiState struct: provider (Arc<Mutex<Box<dyn InferenceProvider>>>), config, app_handle
Create src-tauri/src/ai/config.rs with AiConfig (active_provider, model_path, context_length, temperature, top_p, openai/anthropic fields)
ProviderType enum: Local, OpenAi, Anthropic, Auto
AiConfig::load(app) reads from app_data_dir/ai-config.json, AiConfig::save persists it
init_ai(app) loads config, creates provider, registers AiState
ai_infer command: 60s timeout, 100KB prompt limit
ai_get_config and ai_set_config commands
ai_set_config hot-reloads provider on change
Register all commands in invoke_handler

userStories.title: Ollama HTTP local provider
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a local inference provider that communicates with Ollama via HTTP API.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src-tauri/src/ai/local.rs with LocalProvider struct
Fields: model_path, http client, ollama_endpoint (default localhost:11434), llamacpp_endpoint (default localhost:8080)
infer method: tries Ollama first (POST /api/chat), falls back to llama.cpp server (POST /v1/chat/completions)
Availability check: cached 30s TTL, checks Ollama then llama.cpp
detect_ollama_models: GET /api/tags, returns model name list
check_llamacpp_standalone: GET /health
resolve_model_name: extracts model name from .gguf file path
Implements InferenceProvider trait
cargo check passes

userStories.title: OpenAI and Anthropic cloud providers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need cloud AI providers for OpenAI and Anthropic APIs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src-tauri/src/ai/cloud.rs
OpenAiProvider: configurable endpoint (default api.openai.com/v1), API key, model. POSTs to /chat/completions
AnthropicProvider: API key, model. POSTs to api.anthropic.com/v1/messages with x-api-key and anthropic-version headers
Both implement InferenceProvider trait
Both handle HTTP errors gracefully and return Err with descriptive messages
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Model management backend

acceptanceCriteria: Create src-tauri/src/ai/models.rs with ModelMetadata and ModelInfo structs
ModelManager methods: list_models, scan_models_dir (auto-register .gguf files), register_model, delete_model, switch_model
ai_list_models command: scans models/ dir, cross-validates with DB, marks active model
ai_download_model command: validates HTTPS URL, streams download with progress events (model-download-progress), registers in DB
ai_delete_model command: deletes DB record and file on disk
MAX_MODEL_FILE_SIZE = 5GB validation
ensure_models_dir in db.rs creates models/ subdirectory
Register all commands in invoke_handler
cargo check passes

userStories.title: ComplexityRouter and ai_infer_auto
userStories.description: As a developer, I need intelligent routing that sends simple tasks to local AI and complex tasks to cloud AI.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src-tauri/src/ai/router.rs with Complexity enum (Simple, Medium, Complex)
RoutingDecision struct: provider, complexity, reason
ComplexityRouter::route: analyzes prompt length (<200=Simple, 200-1000=Medium, >1000=Complex) and keywords
Simple+local -> Local, Medium/Complex+cloud -> Cloud, fallback to available provider
ai_infer_auto command: uses router when provider is Auto, otherwise delegates directly
ai_detect_local_provider command: probes Ollama and llama.cpp availability
Register commands in invoke_handler
cargo check passes

userStories.title: AI auto-processing pipeline with type detection and tags
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a background processing queue that automatically detects content type and generates semantic tags for each clipboard item.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src-tauri/src/ai/processor.rs with ProcessingJob (item_id, content, content_type) and AiProcessor (MPSC sender)
start_processor(app): spawns tokio background task with MPSC channel (capacity 100)

Processing pipeline per job: (1) detect_type_rules (JSON/XML/URL/code patterns, AI fallback), (2) generate_tags via ai_infer_auto (3-5 tags), (3) update_ai_metadata_internal to DB, (4) emit 'ai-processed' event
enqueue(processor, job): non-blocking send, drops if channel full
Integration: clipboard.rs calls enqueue after insert_item
Register AiProcessor as Tauri managed state in setup
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Cross-platform build configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the build config verified for macOS, Windows, and Linux with proper platform-specific dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: Cargo.toml: core-graphics only on macOS via [target.'cfg(target_os = "macos")'.dependencies]

npm run build produces dist/ with index.html, CSS, and JS bundles
